name: Tina Smith
subject: Action Needed: Stop Trump's Attorney General nominee
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tina Smith for Minnesota
body:
Here's my stance on this nomination.

| [](https://action.tinaforminnesota. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tsm-email-february?refcode=rtw_20190213_em-header&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=55783&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Sturgeon Lake&state=MN&phone=&employer=&occupation=&recurring=auto") Friend, William Barr, Trump's Attorney General nominee, has an alarming record of abusing civil rights and concerning views on executive power. In his confirmation hearing, Barr repeatedly refused to commit to recusing himself from independent investigations -- even if recommended by ethics officials. He also said he's discussed the Mueller investigation with the White House directly. **I 'm voting no on Barr's confirmation because I believe our next Attorney General must be able to stand up to Trump and act as an independent law enforcement official -- and Barr has proven himself incapable of doing so.** **[Friend, will you add your name now to join me in calling on the Senate to reject this nominee? The Senate will be voting soon, so I need you to join the fight right now.](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc4d02c/c1f7015/614fd193/1140ed69/1906240967/VEsC/ "https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/s/rtw_201902_barr-ld?source=rtw_20190213_em-ld")** [ADD YOUR NAME](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc4d02c/c1f7015/614fd193/1140ed69/1906240967/VEsD/ "https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/s/rtw_201902_barr-ld?source=rtw_20190213_em-ld") The next Attorney General will have great power to oversee important issues including voting rights, criminal justice reform, and the Mueller investigation. Barr is not the right person to hold this office. If enough of us raise our voices, we can stop Barr from being confirmed -- but that will only happen if we all speak up as loudly as we can. name: Debbie Wasserman Schultz
subject: No. More. Shutdowns.
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Debbie Wasserman Schultz for Congress
body:
|
---
| | The latest Trump shutdown didn't only hurt federal workers and their families -- it devastated businesses and our economy. The Congressional Budget Office says the Trump Shutdown **cost us $11 BILLION.** Shutting down the government isn't some kind of negotiating tactic or bargaining chip. It's a failure to govern that negatively impacts the entire county. We cannot let this happen again. [**Today I'm calling on dedicated Democrats (like you) to speak out. Sign on if you agree: Shutdowns are NOT the answer.**](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113cdc/622b7b5b/1eb1b15d/307b7352/2393861896/VEsH/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/ja190205") We clearly can't trust President Trump to avoid another shutdown. The one that just ended was the longest in history and the third during his presidency. **And he's already threatened another when the current deal runs out this month.** Neither Trump, his Cabinet, nor his Republican allies in Congress seem to understand that when they shut down the government, people suffer. Parents can't afford childcare for their kids. People can't afford their rent, their medications, or their everyday necessities. Florida families pay the price for D.C. shutdowns. It's not fair and it shouldn't happen every time Trump decides he wants to waste more taxpayer money. We need to turn up the grassroots pressure to avoid another shutdown. [**Help make it abundantly clear to Trump and Congressional Republicans that we will not tolerate another shutdown.**](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113cdc/622b7b5b/1eb1b15d/307b7352/2393861896/VEsE/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/ja190205") Thank you for joining us. Debbie

name: Kirsten Gillibrand
subject: Enough is enough
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Gillibrand 2020 Exploratory Committee
body:
The gun lobby is not all-powerful.
[](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f9769f1/2ae112ef/176d5f5f/357a8428/1812717716/VEsH/ "https://2020.kirstengillibrand.com/")
Peter,
One year ago today, a gunman walked into Marjory Stoneman Douglas High School and murdered 17 students and educators with an assault weapon.
In the last year, in the wake of unthinkable tragedy, we've seen something incredible emerge--we've seen the group of impossibly brave students in Parkland fearlessly stand up to say, _**" Enough is enough."**_ To speak truth to one of the most powerful lobbies in the country. To spark a movement demanding the change we desperately need.
**If these young people can be so brave, what is stopping Congress from doing the same?** NRA-backed lawmakers have had a year to act--and they've failed. The gun lobby is not all-powerful. They will be defeated--by the young people of Parkland, by families who've lost loved ones and by all of us coming together, refusing to give up in this fight.
**We all need to be as fearless, as unrelenting as the young people of Parkland in demanding a safer, brighter future.** Change will come if we join together and keep fighting for it:
**[If you're looking for a way to help combat our country's gun violence epidemic on this tragic anniversary, please click here to donate to the organizations that are leading this fight to save lives.](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f9769f1/2ae112ef/176d5f5f/357a842b/1812717716/VEsE/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5Ijoia2ctZ3VucmVmb3JtIiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJqdGsyNzc0LWJ1LWdmcC1uZCIsImUzc2dURkJCUVZGMVpYSjVVM1J5YVc1bklIMTkiOiI1LDI1LDUwLDEwMCwyNTAsNTAwIiwiZTNzZ1lXSnlaV04xY25KcGJtY2dmWDA9IjoiIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/kg-gunreform?refcode=jtk2774-bu-gfp-nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")**
The NRA is powerful, but they can't silence us as long as we're fighting together. Thank you for being part of this movement, and thank you to the organizations that are doing this work, day in and day out.
Kirsten
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
[To unsubscribe from the Gillibrand 2020 Exploratory Committee email list, please click here.](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f9769f1/2ae112ef/176d5f5f/357a842a/1812717716/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZGYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://gillibrand.cp.bsd.net/page/unsubscribe/?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com")

subject: Wilbur Ross, Ivanka Trump Introduce Workforce Advisory Board
body:
"The chief executives of Apple Inc., Lockheed Martin Corp. and Walmart Inc., are among 25 prominent Americans who will shape Trump administration..." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=63d776be27&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **Wilbur Ross, Ivanka Trump Introduce Workforce Advisory Board**

“The chief executives of Apple Inc., Lockheed Martin Corp. and Walmart Inc., are among 25 prominent Americans who will shape Trump administration efforts to develop job training programs to meet the changing demands of U.S. employers,” Margaret Talev reports for _Bloomberg_.

“The creation of the American Workforce Policy Advisory Board, announced by Commerce Secretary Wilbur Ross and President Donald Trump’s daughter and adviser Ivanka Trump on Wednesday, will work with the National Council for the American Worker established last July by an executive order.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=bc32783858&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “President Donald Trump is headed to Miami on Monday to speak about the ongoing turmoil in Venezuela, a move that could embolden interim president Juan Guaidó and put further pressure on Nicolás Maduro to flee the country,” David Smiley and Franco Ordoñez reports for the _[Miami Herald](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=eed788548b&e=3ee4985eea)_. “Trump is scheduled to speak at Florida International University, where he’ll reaffirm his support for Guaidó and, according to the White House, hammer socialism as a scourge.”
---
|
---
| In the _[Washington Examiner](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e655390583&e=3ee4985eea)_ , Eddie Scarry writes that what President Trump said is true—he really did start building more border wall in Texas. “The project funds six miles-worth of concrete and steel barrier of the sort that [border] agents told me in January is immensely successful in deterring illegal border crossings. The wall will be supplemented by ‘detection technology, lighting, video surveillance, and an all-weather patrol road parallel’ to the barrier, according to a release sent out by the CBP in November.”
---
|
---
| “I agree with the Trump administration’s free market approach” to healthcare, Dr. Marc Siegel writes in _[The Hill](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d0301ca7a6&e=3ee4985eea)_ _._ "My patients want to know what their medicines cost, even if their insurance ends up covering it. In fact it is ‘price opacity’ that has enabled health costs to soar, while patients are blinded . . . The Trump administration and HHS should be applauded for their efforts to extend and expand price transparency."
---
|

name: Gina Raimondo
subject: Remembering Parkland with Action
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY FRIENDS OF GINA RAIMONDO |
body:
FRIEND,

We’ve all been shaken by one tragic mass shooting after another. Overwhelmingly, Rhode Islanders support sensible laws to protect innocent people against military-style weapons, like the guns used in mass shootings in Parkland, Las Vegas, Orlando and Sandy Hook. There is simply no place for such firepower in civilian life!

Today, on the first anniversary of the horrific Valentine’s Day massacre at a Parkland, Florida high school, I have introduced legislation to ban guns in schools, ban high-capacity magazines, and ban assault weapons like our neighbors have done in Massachusetts and Connecticut.

These bills will keep guns away from schools and make sure weapons of war are no longer in the hands of civilians.

The powerful gun lobby will do everything it can to keep these common-sense reforms from becoming law in Rhode Island. It’s going to be a tough hill to climb to get these bills passed, but that hasn’t stopped us before. Already, we've passed laws allowing guns to be taken from domestic violence convicts, as well as people deemed a high risk.

I will need your help again in the months to come, to finally ban assault weapons and high-capacity magazines in Rhode Island, and to codify our ban on guns in schools. Change is hard, but the cost of doing nothing is too great. Please, work with me to make sure these bills become law. I will let you know when hearings are scheduled, so you can turn out in support of this crucial legislation. Now is the time to get organized and fight for a safer future for our children, and all Rhode Islanders.

Together,

Gina

name: Senator Nina Turner
subject: Medicare for All - Only All That We Love is On the Line
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE SANDERS INSTITUTE
body:
# [](https://sandersinstitute-ourrev.bsd.net/page/m/2ba7a5ea/bed43eb/9c6e7a81/75fbfd21/1404964852/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/sandersinstitute?refcode=email-header")
Dear Friend,
When we talk about healthcare in this country, we must realize that we cannot settle for half-solutions, or half-movements. We must dedicate ourselves to achieving an America that cares for and helps every person, every family, every parent, and every child in this country.... _**because only all that we love is on the line**_.
I recently had the honor of speaking on a National Nurses United panel at the Sanders Institute Gathering that brought together leaders in healthcare movements not only from the United States but from around the world.
**[Watch the Panel](https://sandersinstitute-ourrev.bsd.net/page/m/2ba7a5ea/bed43eb/9c6e7a81/75fbfd20/1404964852/VEsE/ "https://www.sandersinstitute.com/blog/how-we-win-medicare-for-all")**

Kelly Coogan Gehr, Assistant Director of Public Policy and Advocacy for NNU, laid the scene: a righteous movement for healthcare for all.
_" What our enemies most fear is this: Medicare for All gives a fifth of our economy back to the people and increases the size, strength, and power of our democratic government." - Kelly Coogan Gehr_
[](https://sandersinstitute-ourrev.bsd.net/page/m/2ba7a5ea/bed43eb/9c6e7a81/75fbfd20/1404964852/VEsF/ "https://www.sandersinstitute.com/blog/how-we-win-medicare-for-all")
Dr. Diane Archer, President of Just Care USA, explained why Medicare for All is the best and only option - because we cannot encourage a "middle-ground" system that would continue to include private companies that exclude patients that need care to maximize their profits while hiking up costs for everybody else.
_" Commercial insurers drive up administrative costs and pocket profits for their shareholders rather than investing in their members' needs, let alone the public health." - Dr. Diane Archer_
Jean Ross, Nurse and Co-President of National Nurses United, through Kelly Coogan Gehr spoke about the moral imperative behind a Medicare for All system and why our strength not only lies with the statistics, and the economic benefits, but also in the moral imperative of healthcare as a human right.
_" One's ability to stay alive should never be based in someone else's ability to profit." - Jean Ross_
[](https://sandersinstitute-ourrev.bsd.net/page/m/2ba7a5ea/bed43eb/9c6e7a81/75fbfd20/1404964852/VEsC/ "https://www.sandersinstitute.com/blog/how-we-win-medicare-for-all")
Dr. Hosnieh Djafari-Marbini described the reverence for and popularity of the UK's single payer system but warned of spreading privatization - an import from the American system - that is jeopardizing the popular and quality care that has been provided in the UK for over 70 years.
_" It is no exaggeration to say that The NHS in the UK is revered and is more of a national obsession than a great cup of tea or in fact the weather. It is revered as it provides that most basic of human rights; free universal healthcare at your time of need." - Dr. Hosnieh Djafari-Marbini_
Jo Beardsmore, co-founder of UK Uncut, convinced every person in the room that we must not only fight for healthcare for all but that we can win!
_" Some people seem to think that to aim for an expanded and improved Medicare for All is too ambitious. But we know that it is only by aiming for such an ideal that we will mobilize enough people to take action to help us win. If we don't aim high, we will fall. low, and will become compromised before we even start to negotiate. Big organizing techniques that NNU is using to make the movement happen." - Jo Beardsmore_
[](https://sandersinstitute-ourrev.bsd.net/page/m/2ba7a5ea/bed43eb/9c6e7a81/75fbfd20/1404964852/VEsD/ "https://www.sandersinstitute.com/blog/how-we-win-medicare-for-all")
Finally, Libby Devlin, Southern Director of NNU, showed us that nurses in each and every state in the U.S. and around the country are already leading the charge because they know - and see every day - what is at stake.
_"__Every day_ _nurses see the damage done by a lack of health care. They, better than anyone, know that a single standard of care that is safe, effective and therapeutic, regardless of ability to pay must become the public policy of this country "- Libby Devlin_
The voices of the nurses in our country are important. They care for patients in their greatest time of need. As the healing hands at the bedside, they have been the consistent witnesses to the inequality of our current health care system. They bear witness to the need for systemic change. They believe as I do that our collective activism is the catalyst for change.
Medicare for All is possible.
It is right.
It is within our reach.
And we must reach for it... because _**only all that we love is on the line**_.
In Solidarity,
Senator Nina Turner
Founding Fellow, The Sanders Institute

subject: What we have to do to win
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Gillibrand 2020 Exploratory Committee
body:
Friday marks one month since Kirsten announced she was running.
| | | Hey Peter!

subject: One year after Parkland
body:
On February 14, 2018, a horrific act of violence took the lives of 14 students and 3 educators. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=3d8aa28025&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • February 14, 2019
---
| | |
# **One year after Parkland**

**On February 14, 2018,** a horrific act of violence took the lives of 14 students and 3 educators on the campus of Marjory Stoneman Douglas High School in Parkland, Florida. On this somber anniversary, we remember 17 lives that ended far too soon—and recommit ourselves to ensuring the safety of all Americans, especially our children.

**“We cannot imagine** the sorrow and suffering the Parkland families have endured. Our entire Nation mourns for the victims and their loved ones, and we pledge our unwavering resolve to work with the leaders in this room to secure our Nation’s schools and everywhere else,” [President Donald J. Trump said last night.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=0ac5ca3fd9&e=3ee4985eea)

**Within days** of the Parkland tragedy, President Trump convened a listening session with families, teachers, and student survivors of gun violence at the White House. “We learned a lot that afternoon,” the President wrote in a statement today.

**In the year since Parkland** , important strides have been made. One month after that listening session, President Trump signed the STOP School Violence Act and Fix NICS Act into law, which provides grants to improve school safety and strengthens background checks for firearm purchases. The Administration has also completed a regulatory process, begun in October 2017, to ban bump stocks.

**“Melania and I** join all Americans in praying for the continued healing of those in the Parkland community and all communities where lives have been lost to gun violence,” President Trump said in his statement. “Today, as we hold in our hearts each of those lost a year ago in Parkland, let us declare together, as Americans, that we will not rest until our schools are secure and our communities are safe.”

[Presidential message on remembering the Parkland tragedy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=245beb66a5&e=3ee4985eea)

**What you should know:** [How President Trump is making our schools safer](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=7a9c9c6cdc&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## **For Valentine’s Day, First Lady brings joy to kids**

**Earlier today,** First Lady Melania Trump visited the Children’s Inn at the National Institutes of Health (NIH) for an afternoon of Valentine’s Day festivities with some young patients.

**The day was filled** making candy grams, snow globes shaped like hearts, and other arts and crafts. She also hand-delivered a White House valentine to each of the children.

**The****well-being** of our Nation’s children is a top priority for the First Lady, as exemplified by her Be Best initiative. Yesterday, while hosting Colombian First Lady Maria Juliana Ruiz Sandoval at the White House, she discussed the devastating impact that the opioid crisis is having on children and young mothers.

**“****My conversation** with the First Lady of Colombia today further encouraged a global mission to fighting the addiction crisis and issues facing children domestically and all around the world,” the First Lady said in a statement.

[First Lady Melania Trump meets with the First Lady of Colombia.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b1e997a2b7&e=3ee4985eea)

**Learn more:** [The Be Best initiative puts our children first](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=9144238e32&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|

name: Kamala Harris
subject: BREAKING: Trump declares national emergency
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY KAMALA HARRIS FOR THE PEOPLE
body:
|
#
Just a few moments ago, Mitch McConnell announced on the Senate floor that President Trump will sign an emergency declaration in order to secure funding for his useless and costly border wall. This comes after both Congress and the American people have repeatedly made clear that they do not support building this wall. **I want to be clear with you -- this is ridiculous and I am going to do everything in my power to stop Trump from building this wall. It’s a waste of your money and it won’t make our country safer.** But I cannot do it alone. I need you with me in this fight: **[If you’re with me, add your name to my urgent petition opposing the President’s decision to declare a national emergency in a blatantly political attempt to build his vanity project.](https://action.kamalaharris.org/sign/no-border-wall?firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&refcode=ema190214-full&source=ema190214-full&t=1&akid=3085%2E5755789%2EL3MJF4)** | | [Sign the petition](https://action.kamalaharris.org/sign/no-border-wall?firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&refcode=ema190214-full&source=ema190214-full&t=2&akid=3085%2E5755789%2EL3MJF4)
---
We should be doing something about the actual emergencies plaguing our nation, like the gun violence epidemic or health care access, not playing politics in order to build a ridiculously wasteful border wall.
I am ready to fight with everything I have. I hope you’re with me.
— Kamala
